repo_prior_art.cite: pid_manski1990test — selected-cell Manski-bound tightening, a different model and target

published_prior_art.cite: tan_blanchet_syrgkanis_2026_prte_ot, Remark 3.3 — explicitly anticipates a sharp joint-kernel CCOT extension for bounded joint costs, but does not pursue it

per_theorem_calls.label: Proposition 1 — joint frontier and dispersion ratio

published_prior_art.cite: firpo_ridder_2019_ted_functionals — joint treatment-effect-distribution feasibility is not the Cartesian product of pointwise bounds; Frandsen and Lefgren (2021) optimize over structured copulas
ober_reynolds_2023_joint_po_ot — dual OT estimators for joint-potential-outcome bounds are root-n under regularity, but not with the proposed generated Roy constraint map

novelty_flags.label: Theorem 1 and related work
novelty_flags.one_line: Han and Yang (2024) is a closer omitted PRTE paper: it computes sharp nonparametric bounds under IV independence and a menu of identifying restrictions; Gao, Ge, and Qian (2025) is also a missing exact causal-MOT identified-set comparator.

novelty_flags.label: Theorem 1 / comparator_promise_table
novelty_flags.one_line: Calling the result a strict tightening of Tan et al. and Fan et al. is unsupported: it changes the target and adds rank/copula restrictions rather than proving a strict containment or frontier against either published result on a common model.

novelty_flags.label: firpo_ridder_2019_ted_functionals
novelty_flags.one_line: The cited bibliographic object is wrong: Firpo and Ridder's 2019 paper is 'Partial identification of the treatment effect distribution and its functionals,' Journal of Econometrics 213, 210–234, not Journal of Econometrics 211(1), 1–22.

soundness_flags.label: lem:factual-map and thm:sharp-common-coupling
soundness_flags.one_line: A_X r gives P(Y=y_i,D=d|Z=z_ell,X=x), whereas b_P(x)=E[s(W)|X=x] equals P(Z=z_ell,Y=y_i,D=d|X=x); their coordinates differ by P(Z=z_ell|X=x), so the load-bearing equality A_Xr=b_P is false.

soundness_flags.label: target_estimand
soundness_flags.one_line: P is declared to be the observed-data law of W, but psi(P) integrates latent U and potential outcomes; define the target on a compatible structural law Q and define its identified set conditional on P.

soundness_flags.label: prop:joint-frontier-and-ratio
soundness_flags.one_line: The proposal defines R_epsilon(P,x), not a single polytope R_epsilon(P,X); with unrestricted X the feasible object is a measurable-selection set, so the claimed linear image of a single polytope is undefined.

soundness_flags.label: prop:joint-frontier-and-ratio
soundness_flags.one_line: The universal claim that the sharp multi-band set is not a Cartesian product fails for M=1 and for degenerate restrictions that force a singleton or a rectangular image; it needs explicit nonrectangularity conditions or a witness.

soundness_flags.label: prop:joint-frontier-and-ratio
soundness_flags.one_line: Absent a strict nonrectangularity theorem, the multi-band image statement is a definition of joint feasibility and the ratio equality is the standard Charnes--Cooper reparameterization, not a substantive kernel.

soundness_flags.label: oeq:orthogonal-dual-inference
soundness_flags.one_line: L2 convergence of a grid-valued propensity estimator does not control changes in A_X, which jumps when an estimated propensity crosses a cutpoint; add exact-grid recovery or a margin/stability condition before claiming a root-n score route.

soundness_flags.label: ass:dml-product-rate
soundness_flags.one_line: The displayed joint o_P(n^{-1/4}) rate is stronger than the standard DML product-rate condition cited to Chernozhukov et al. (2018), and it does not address the discontinuous propensity-to-constraint map; retag and justify it or state the appropriate product and grid-stability conditions.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: thm:sharp-common-coupling
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: The rank-shape and copula restrictions are maintained sensitivity assumptions, not empirically identified features of the unrestricted gain distribution.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: The gain-band interval is sharp conditional on the stated rank-structured polyhedral coupling class.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Rebuild the observable compatibility vector as b_{d,ell,i}(x)=P(Y=y_i,D=d|Z=z_ell,X=x), or multiply each A_X coordinate by P(Z=z_ell|X=x), and state the target on a compatible structural law rather than the observed law.
Replace the arbitrary H r≤h_epsilon umbrella with one concrete, motivated copula restriction and prove a strict identified-set comparison against Tan et al. on a shared observable model.
State explicit conditions and a finite worked witness under which the multi-band image is strictly nonrectangular; otherwise demote the Charnes--Cooper observation to a computational remark.

For inference, impose grid-classification stability or treat propensities as known, then derive the full score including instrument-cell probabilities and compare with Firpo--Galvao--Parker value-function inference.

recommended_next_step: Return to Stage -1 and rebuild the observed-data map and target-law distinction before reconsidering this angle; do not advance this version to Stage 0.
